metadata.vintage: Curated from each jurisdiction's published affordable-housing ordinance, IGA, or property-tax abatement program page. Verify before relying on for an underwrite — programs change yearly.
metadata.scope: Top 30 CO jurisdictions where affordable housing is actively developing. Smaller jurisdictions fall back to county / state programs.

categories.property-tax-exemption: Statutory or local-ordinance exemption from property tax for qualifying affordable rental properties (often tied to 60% AMI cap + LURA).
categories.PILOT: Payment in Lieu of Taxes — negotiated agreement reducing property tax in exchange for affordability commitments.
categories.fee-waiver: Reduction or waiver of impact fees, plan-review fees, building permit fees, water/sewer tap fees, parkland dedication.
categories.expedited-review: Priority / expedited entitlement track for qualifying affordable projects (saves carrying cost).
categories.density-bonus: Additional units / FAR / height in exchange for affordability set-asides.
categories.land-contribution: Public land donated or sold below market for affordable development.
categories.URA-TIF: Urban Renewal Authority / Tax Increment Financing district that can capture incremental taxes for affordable.
categories.linkage-fee: Inclusionary or linkage fee on market-rate development that funds affordable trust funds.

state_baseline.note: Colorado statute C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 grants property tax exemption to affordable-rental properties owned by 501(c)(3) nonprofits + maintained at ≤60% AMI per a recorded LURA. Most large rental affordable deals qualify automatically.

programs.name: Denver Affordable Housing Property Tax Exemption (501(c)(3) per C.R.S. 39-3-112.5)
programs.summary: Full property tax exemption for qualifying nonprofit-owned affordable rental properties at ≤60% AMI. Critical underwriting assumption: full exemption boosts NOI ~15-20% vs. taxable comp.
programs.magnitude: 100% of ad valorem property tax (typical $0.50-$1.00/sf/yr in Denver)

programs.name: Dedicated Affordable Housing Fund (DAHF) — Linkage Fee + 0.5 mill sales tax
programs.summary: Self-funding source ~$30M/yr for affordable gap financing. Linkage on new market-rate development per CRS §29-26-101.
programs.magnitude: Up to ~$25M/yr available via competitive RFPs

programs.name: Denver Use-Tax + Plan-Review Fee Waiver for affordable
programs.summary: Selected fee waivers for projects with affordability covenants ≥30 years; ranges $50K-$300K/project.

programs.name: C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 (statewide; default for 501(c)(3) deals in Aurora)
programs.summary: Aurora has no city-specific property tax abatement; the statewide nonprofit exemption is the operative tool.
programs.magnitude: 100% city property tax (limited; CO local property tax is mostly county + special districts)

programs.name: Aurora Affordable Housing Fee Waiver Program
programs.summary: Building permit + plan-review fee reduction for qualifying affordable projects.

programs.name: Boulder Inclusionary Housing Ordinance
programs.summary: Requires 25% of new residential to be permanently affordable OR equivalent cash-in-lieu (typically $50-$100/sf). Funds Boulder's Affordable Housing Fund.
programs.magnitude: Cash-in-lieu rate varies; current $100/sf for new construction

programs.name: Affordable Housing Permit + Tap Fee Waiver
programs.summary: Plan-review + water/sewer tap fees waived for projects at ≤60% AMI with permanent affordability covenants.

programs.name: C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 statutory exemption
programs.summary: Default statutory exemption for 501(c)(3)-owned ≤60% AMI.

programs.name: Colorado Springs Affordable Housing Fee Waiver
programs.summary: Reduction or waiver of impact fees + plan-review fees for projects ≤80% AMI; case-by-case.

programs.name: Colorado Springs URA project areas
programs.summary: Active in city-designated blighted areas; can capture tax increment to fund affordable as part of larger redevelopment.

programs.name: Pueblo Urban Renewal Authority project areas
programs.summary: Several active project areas with TIF; PUR can negotiate developer-friendly tax increment dedications for affordable.

programs.name: C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 statutory exemption
programs.summary: Default for 501(c)(3) ≤60% AMI.

programs.name: Fort Collins Affordable Housing Fee Credit Program
programs.summary: Credits / reductions on capital expansion fees + utility plant investment fees for affordable projects ≤80% AMI.
programs.magnitude: Up to $15K-$25K per unit in fee reductions

programs.name: Fort Collins Affordable Housing Capital Fund
programs.summary: City general fund + dedicated revenue for gap financing.

programs.name: Greeley Affordable Housing Fee Waiver / Reduction
programs.summary: Reduction of impact + utility tap fees for qualifying affordable projects.

programs.name: Longmont Inclusionary Housing + Cash-in-Lieu
programs.summary: 12% inclusionary on new ownership; cash-in-lieu fund affordable rental gap. Mandatory.

programs.name: Longmont Affordable Housing Fee Subsidy
programs.summary: Plan review + capital expansion fee credits for ≤60% AMI projects.

programs.name: Lakewood Inclusionary Housing Ordinance (Strategic Growth Initiative)
programs.summary: Affordable housing requirement on larger new developments; cash-in-lieu funds city affordable trust.

programs.name: Vail InDEED + Eagle Valley Workforce Housing programs
programs.summary: Vail InDEED uses dedicated funds to buy deed restrictions on existing units (purchase-side workforce housing). Eagle Co also offers fee waivers + density bonuses for projects ≥80% AMI deed restricted.

programs.name: Eagle County / Town of Vail land donations
programs.summary: Periodic public-land contributions for resort workforce housing — Timber Ridge, EverVail.

programs.name: Vail commercial linkage + employee housing requirements
programs.summary: Commercial development must mitigate new employee housing demand via build/buy/fee.

programs.name: Aspen-Pitkin County Housing Authority (APCHA) — affordable housing mitigation
programs.summary: Highest-impact program in CO: mandatory mitigation on commercial + residential development funds APCHA workforce-housing portfolio (~3,000 units). Cash-in-lieu rates among highest in nation.

programs.name: Aspen / Pitkin County land contributions
programs.summary: Periodic public-land donations; recent: Burlingame Ranch + Lumberyard.

programs.name: C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 (statewide statute is the operative tool)
programs.summary: Garfield County towns generally don't run city-specific abatement programs; the statewide nonprofit exemption is the workhorse.

programs.name: Town-by-town impact + tap fee discretion
programs.summary: Glenwood, Carbondale, Rifle have negotiated case-by-case fee reductions on selected affordable projects; no published formal programs as of 2026-06. Verify via Town Manager.

programs.name: Summit Combined Housing Authority (SCHA) mitigation + linkage
programs.summary: County + municipal partnership; commercial linkage funds workforce housing programs across the county.

programs.name: Summit County / Town land contributions
programs.summary: Smith Ranch (Silverthorne), Wellington (Breckenridge) — recent public-land affordable deals.

programs.name: Telluride affordable housing mitigation
programs.summary: Mandatory affordable mitigation on commercial + residential development; very strong inclusionary requirements.

programs.name: Yampa Valley Housing Authority + Steamboat linkage
programs.summary: Active local housing authority; some commercial linkage + city-funded gap financing.

programs.name: Broomfield affordable housing fee reductions
programs.summary: Plan-review + impact fee reductions case-by-case for affordable.

programs.name: Loveland Affordable Housing Fee Credit
programs.summary: Capital expansion fee credits for affordable projects.

programs.name: Estes Park workforce-housing program
programs.summary: Town has explored linkage + deed restrictions; verify current program status.

programs.name: Durango affordable housing fee reduction
programs.summary: City has reduced fees for selected affordable projects; no formal published schedule.

programs.name: C.R.S. §39-3-112.5 (default)
programs.summary: Grand Junction relies on the statewide statutory exemption; no city-specific abatement program.

programs.name: Wheat Ridge Affordable Housing Fee Adjustment
programs.summary: City offers selective fee adjustments for qualifying affordable projects through community development. Case-by-case.

programs.name: Arvada Urban Renewal Authority project areas
programs.summary: Active project areas with TIF capacity for affordable development partnerships.

programs.name: Arvada Affordable Housing Fee Adjustment
programs.summary: Selective impact + plan-review fee adjustments for affordable projects.

programs.name: Aurora Affordable Housing Fee Waiver Program
programs.summary: Building permit + plan-review fee reduction for qualifying affordable projects.

programs.name: Aurora Urban Renewal Authority
programs.summary: Active in city-designated areas; tax increment financing capacity for affordable as part of larger redevelopment.

programs.name: Parker Affordable Housing Fee Reduction
programs.summary: Town offers selective fee reductions for affordable projects meeting income targeting.

programs.name: Castle Rock Affordable Housing Fee Adjustment
programs.summary: Selective fee adjustments for projects with permanent affordability covenants.

programs.name: Loveland Affordable Housing Fee Credit
programs.summary: Capital expansion fee credits for affordable projects.

programs.name: Loveland URA project areas
programs.summary: Active urban renewal districts can capture TIF for affordable as part of mixed-use redevelopment.

programs.name: Vail InDEED Program
programs.summary: Town pays property owners cash in exchange for recording a permanent deed restriction (occupancy + appreciation cap) on existing market-rate condos. Owner keeps full title.
programs.magnitude: Town pays ~$200K+ per unit (~10-25% of market value). 220+ units in portfolio as of 2024.

programs.name: Golden Affordable Housing Fee Adjustment
programs.summary: Selective impact + plan-review fee adjustments for qualifying affordable projects.

programs.name: Brighton Affordable Housing Fee Reduction
programs.summary: Plan review + capital expansion fee reductions for affordable projects.
